title: Decision Flow in Strictly Competitive Games
description: A flowchart illustrating the process of finding a maximin strategy in a two-player, zero-sum game, highlighting the concept of maximization against a rational opponent.
elements: Identify Minimum Payoff per Row (Security Level)
Select the Row with the Maximum Minimum Payoff (Maximin Value)
Saddle Point / Value of the Game
relationships: Player 1 analyzes their payoff matrix to find the worst-case payoff (minimum) for each possible strategy
Player 1 then chooses the strategy that maximizes this worst-case payoff (the maximin strategy)
In a zero-sum game, Player 2's goal is to minimize Player 1's maximum gain (minimax strategy)
If the maximin value equals the minimax value, this is the value of the game and defines a saddle point equilibrium
